summary: This PR adds a minimal in-memory token-bucket rate limiter in a new `examples/token_bucket.py` file. The `TokenBucket` class tracks per-key (token, timestamp) state in a plain dict and computes refills lazily on each `allow()` call using `time.monotonic()`, avoiding any background thread. The deliberate design trade-offs — no eviction, no persistence, single-process only — are documented in the module docstring, making this a focused, self-contained example of the token-bucket algorithm rather than a production-grade library.
